prevalence.source: WHO/CDC-informed prevalence range + Brazilian service utilization context
prevalence.trend: increasing due to improved recognition and diagnostic expansion

incidence.value: 3-6 per 1,000 live births (diagnosed over early childhood)

burden.mortalityRate: Low; indirect risks via comorbidities and accidents

vulnerablePopulations: children with limited access to early intervention services
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: persistent social communication deficits and restricted/repetitive behaviors with early developmental onset; diagnosis often supported by standardized tools (e.g., ADOS) when available

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; developmental screening in APS is inconsistent
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: hundreds of thousands to >1 million children (depending on ascertainment)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Not applicable; focus is on functional outcomes and access to early intervention

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist assessment (neuropediatrics/psychology) concentrated in larger cities; APS often initiates referral based on developmental concerns
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Early behavioral interventions, speech therapy, occupational therapy, caregiver training, and school accommodations; medication for comorbid irritability/ADHD when indicated
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Long wait times, limited specialized teams in SUS, and unequal access to ABA/specialized therapies
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Increasing public awareness and expanding multidisciplinary services in some urban centers
